title: Hobbies & Leisure
shortSummary: Talk about your hobbies, free time, and what you like to do in Turkish.
culturalNote: Leisure in Turkey is deeply social, often built around long conversations over tea or Turkish coffee in a cafe or at home. Football (futbol) is a national passion, and the big Istanbul clubs inspire fierce, friendly loyalty; asking someone hangi takımı tutuyorsun? (which team do you support?) can spark a lively chat. Backgammon (tavla) is played everywhere in tea houses, accompanied by good-natured banter. Many Turks enjoy walks along the seaside (sahil), picnics in the countryside, and visiting family at weekends. Reading, music, and increasingly going to the gym are popular too. To say you like an activity, Turkish adds the accusative to the noun before sevmek (to love), as in müziği seviyorum (I love music), and to say you can do something it uses the ability suffix -ebil, as in yüzebilirim (I can swim).

keyPhrases.english: I like going to the cinema

grammar.explanation: To say you like doing an activity, turn the verb into a noun with -mek/-mak, add the accusative -i, and follow with sevmek (to love): yüzmeyi seviyorum (I love swimming), okumayı seviyorum (I love reading). The verb becomes the object of 'love'.
grammar.commonMistake: Leaving the verb in its plain form. It is yüzmeyi seviyorum, not yüzmek seviyorum.

grammar.explanation: To say you can or are able to do something, insert -ebil or -abil (with vowel harmony) before the personal ending: yüzebilirim (I can swim), gelebilirim (I can come), oynayabilirim (I can play). The negative 'cannot' uses -emez/-amaz: yüzemem (I can't swim).
grammar.commonMistake: Forgetting vowel harmony in the suffix. After a back vowel it is oynayabilirim, not oynayebilirim.

dialogue.setup: Ece and Kerem chat about their hobbies and plan a weekend activity.

lines.speaker: Ece
lines.english: Kerem, what do you do in your free time?

lines.speaker: Ece
lines.native: Ben müzik dinlemeyi ve kitap okumayı severim.
lines.english: I love listening to music and reading books.

lines.speaker: Ece
lines.english: Yes, I love swimming a lot.

lines.speaker: Kerem
lines.native: O zaman hafta sonu sahile gidelim mi?
lines.english: Then shall we go to the seaside at the weekend?

quiz.question: How do you say 'I read books' in Turkish?
quiz.explanation: Kitap is book; okuyorum is 'I am reading'.

quiz.question: Which phrase means 'I love swimming'?
quiz.explanation: Yüzmeyi is the object form of 'swimming'; seviyorum is 'I love'.

quiz.question: Gitar çal_____. (I can play guitar)
quiz.explanation: The ability suffix -abil plus -irim gives çalabilirim, 'I can play'.

quiz.question: What does 'Hangi takımı tutuyorsun?' ask?
quiz.explanation: Takım is team; tutmak (to hold) here means 'to support'.

quiz.question: Arrange: gidelim / beraber / mi
quiz.explanation: Beraber gidelim mi? means 'Shall we go together?'
